title: Full closure report — on-time, on-budget project
description: Generate a complete closure report for a project that finished on schedule and within budget, verifying that all four agents produce comprehensive outputs.

input.context: All deliverables accepted by steering committee. Three vendor contracts (infrastructure, QA tooling, data migration) all fulfilled. Operations team onboarded via 4-week shadowing. No open risks or issues.

expected_outputs.deliverable_acceptance_audit: Complete acceptance matrix showing 24/24 deliverables accepted with sign-off references and zero remediation items.
expected_outputs.performance_summary: SPI and CPI both near 1.0, scope at 100%, quality KPIs met. Minimal variance analysis required. Positive trend narrative.
expected_outputs.contract_closure: All 3 contracts closed with completion certificates, final invoices reconciled, vendor performance scorecards rated satisfactory or above.
expected_outputs.knowledge_transfer: Handoff package delivered, operations team sign-off obtained, lessons learned captured including factors that contributed to on-time delivery.

evaluation_criteria: All four agent outputs are present and complete
Performance metrics accurately reflect on-time, on-budget status
Acceptance matrix shows 100% acceptance with no gaps
Contract closure matrix shows all contracts formally closed
Knowledge transfer plan includes validation evidence
Lessons learned capture positive practices for replication

title: Closure for descoped project — partial delivery
description: Generate a closure report for a project that was descoped mid-execution due to budget constraints, resulting in partial delivery of originally committed scope.

input.context: At month 7, steering committee approved CR-041 removing the mobile app module and advanced analytics dashboard due to budget reallocation. 8 deliverables formally descoped. 2 vendor contracts required amendment. Remaining 17 deliverables accepted. Operations handoff complicated by reduced functionality requiring updated documentation.

expected_outputs.deliverable_acceptance_audit: Matrix showing 17/25 accepted, 8 explicitly marked as descoped with CR-041 reference. Zero items in ambiguous state.
expected_outputs.performance_summary: Pre-descope and post-descope baselines compared. CPI favorable due to savings. SPI shows delay before re-baseline. Variance analysis explains descope decision and its cascading effects.
expected_outputs.contract_closure: 3 contracts closed normally, 2 contracts amended and closed with revised scope. Financial reconciliation reflects contract value adjustments.
expected_outputs.knowledge_transfer: Handoff documentation reflects delivered (reduced) scope. Explicit documentation of what was NOT delivered and why, to prevent operational confusion.

evaluation_criteria: Descoped items are explicitly tracked, not silently omitted
Change request CR-041 is referenced as the authorizing artifact
Performance metrics show both original and re-baselined comparisons
Contract amendments are documented with financial impact
Knowledge transfer materials accurately reflect reduced scope
Report clearly communicates what remains undelivered for future planning

title: Closure for cancelled project — proper documentation
description: Generate a closure report for a project that was cancelled at 40% completion due to a strategic pivot, ensuring all administrative, financial, and contractual obligations are properly documented and closed.

input.cancellation_reason: Strategic pivot to SaaS acquisition model — build decision reversed by CTO
input.context: CTO decision memo dated 2026-01-15 cancels the project effective 2026-02-01. Two vendors have early termination clauses; two do not and require negotiation. 9 deliverables in progress have partial work products. Architecture and design artifacts from Phase 1 have reuse value for the SaaS evaluation.

expected_outputs.deliverable_acceptance_audit: 6 accepted, 9 in-progress with partial work product inventory, 17 not started. All work products cataloged for potential reuse.
expected_outputs.performance_summary: Performance at cancellation point: metrics through 40% completion. Sunk cost analysis. Salvageable asset inventory with estimated reuse value.
expected_outputs.contract_closure: 2 contracts closed via early termination clause, 2 contracts requiring negotiated exit with estimated settlement costs. Financial exposure summary.
expected_outputs.knowledge_transfer: Capture of Phase 1 architecture decisions and design artifacts for SaaS evaluation team. Lessons learned focused on build-vs-buy decision factors.

evaluation_criteria: Cancellation is treated as a formal closure event, not an abandonment
CTO decision memo is referenced as the authorizing artifact
In-progress work products are inventoried with reuse potential assessed
Contracts without early termination clauses are flagged with financial exposure
Sunk cost is calculated and reported without judgment
Knowledge transfer focuses on preserving reusable assets and informing the SaaS evaluation

title: Agile project closure with benefits realization tracking setup
description: Generate a closure report for an Agile project delivered iteratively, including setup of post-project benefits realization tracking to measure whether intended business outcomes are achieved.

expected_benefits: 40% reduction in claims processing time within 6 months
25% reduction in manual review workload within 3 months
15% improvement in claims accuracy within 12 months
Net cost savings of USD 2.8M annually by month 18
input.context: Delivered via 5 Program Increments with quarterly inspect-and-adapt cycles. 15 low-priority stories deprioritized by Product Owner and deferred to BAU backlog. All epics and capabilities completed. Two vendor contracts (AI/ML platform license, cloud infrastructure). Benefits realization requires post-project measurement plan with defined KPIs, data sources, measurement frequency, and accountable owners.

expected_outputs.deliverable_acceptance_audit: Capability-level acceptance (not story-level) mapped to epic completion. 287/302 stories delivered; 15 deferred to BAU backlog with Product Owner acknowledgment.
expected_outputs.performance_summary: Agile metrics: velocity trends, PI predictability scores, defect rates, story completion ratios. Budget variance of +4% explained. Scope managed through backlog prioritization rather than formal change requests.
expected_outputs.contract_closure: AI/ML platform license transitioned to operational subscription. Cloud infrastructure contract closed with handoff to FinOps team.
expected_outputs.knowledge_transfer: Benefits Realization Tracking Plan with KPI definitions, baseline measurements, target values, data sources, measurement schedule, and accountable business owners for each benefit. Operational handoff for BAU backlog management.

evaluation_criteria: Agile metrics are used instead of traditional EVM where appropriate
Story-level tracking is aggregated to capability or epic level for closure
Deprioritized stories are tracked as backlog deferrals, not scope failures
Benefits realization plan includes measurable KPIs with baselines and targets
Each benefit has an accountable owner and measurement schedule defined
Contract closure addresses subscription and operational cost transitions
Knowledge transfer includes BAU backlog handoff to product management
